key_insight: GAIA learns geometry-aware operator mappings by turning boundaries and interior fields into tokens, then using cross-attention to adapt integral kernels so one model can solve forward, boundary value, and inverse PDE problems on arbitrary domains.
engineering_relevance: This could reduce the need for task-specific solvers, retraining, and mesh/graph engineering in scientific simulation workflows across complex geometries.
method: Encodes domain geometry and field data into a shared set of geometry tokens.
Conditions integral transform layers on these tokens with cross-attention for local geometric adaptation.
Uses one architecture for forward, BVP, and inverse problems without iterative optimization or graph construction.
evidence_signal: Evaluated on seven 2D/3D benchmarks, including four new or extended inverse/BVP tasks, and reported state-of-the-art results on every inverse and BVP case plus strong forward-problem performance.
watch_item: Performance gains depend on reliable geometry tokenization and may still need careful handling of resolution changes or domain-specific benchmark shifts in deployment.
paper_title: GAIA: Geometry-Adaptive Operator Learning for Forward and Inverse Problems
